conditions.column_name: Macherey-Nagel 250 mm × 4.6 mm Nucleosil C8 HD

field_evidence.critical_condition_basis: The optimum eluent is a mixture of 58.05% chloroform, 6.45% methanol, and 35.50% n-heptane (v/v/v) using a reverse phase (C8) column.
field_evidence.critical_component: The earliest practical examples of LCCC were focusing on endgroup separations.
field_evidence.column_name: The following columns were used: Macherey-Nagel 250 mm × 4.6 mm Nucleosil C8 HD, pore diameter of 100 Å, particle size of 3 μm and VWR 250 mm × 4.6 mm Nucleosil C8, pore diameter of 120 Å, particle size of 3 μm.
field_evidence.mobile_phase_solvents: The mobile phase, a mixture of three organics solvents (58.05 vol % of chloroform, 6.45 vol % of methanol, and 35.50 vol % of n-heptane, SDS HPLC grade), was delivered by a Waters 600 pump at a flow rate of 0.8 mL min−1.
field_evidence.mobile_phase_ratio: The optimum eluent is a mixture of 58.05% chloroform, 6.45% methanol, and 35.50% n-heptane (v/v/v) using a reverse phase (C8) column.
field_evidence.temperature_celsius: They were placed in a thermostated Crococil oven (Polymer Laboratories), in which a temperature of 30 °C was maintained for all measurements.
field_evidence.pore_size: The following columns were used: Macherey-Nagel 250 mm × 4.6 mm Nucleosil C8 HD, pore diameter of 100 Å, particle size of 3 μm and VWR 250 mm × 4.6 mm Nucleosil C8, pore diameter of 120 Å, particle size of 3 μm.
field_evidence.flow_rate: The mobile phase, a mixture of three organics solvents (58.05 vol % of chloroform, 6.45 vol % of methanol, and 35.50 vol % of n-heptane, SDS HPLC grade), was delivered by a Waters 600 pump at a flow rate of 0.8 mL min−1.

conditions.physical_address: Aix-Marseille Univ, CNRS, Institut de Chimie Radicalaire, UMR 7273, Faculte de Saint-Jerome, avenue Escadrille Normandie-Niemen, service 542, 13397 Marseille cedex 20, France

conditions.column_name: Macherey-Nagel 250 mm × 4.6 mm Nucleosil C8 HD

field_evidence.critical_condition_basis: The optimum eluent is a mixture of 58.05% chloroform, 6.45% methanol, and 35.50% n-heptane (v/v/v) using a reverse phase (C8) column.
field_evidence.critical_component: The calibration curve obtained using PS homopolymer standards could be used to determine the whole molar mass of the PS block in block copolymers with PS and PEO segments, with a weak influence of the architecture.
field_evidence.column_name: The following columns were used: Macherey-Nagel 250 mm × 4.6 mm Nucleosil C8 HD, pore diameter of 100 Å, particle size of 3 μm and VWR 250 mm × 4.6 mm Nucleosil C8, pore diameter of 120 Å, particle size of 3 μm.

field_evidence.temperature_celsius: They were placed in a thermostated Crococil oven (Polymer Laboratories), in which a temperature of 30 °C was maintained for all measurements.
field_evidence.pore_size: The following columns were used: Macherey-Nagel 250 mm × 4.6 mm Nucleosil C8 HD, pore diameter of 100 Å, particle size of 3 μm and VWR 250 mm × 4.6 mm Nucleosil C8, pore diameter of 120 Å, particle size of 3 μm.
field_evidence.flow_rate: The mobile phase, a mixture of three organics solvents (58.05 vol % of chloroform, 6.45 vol % of methanol, and 35.50 vol % of n-heptane, SDS HPLC grade), was delivered by a Waters 600 pump at a flow rate of 0.8 mL min−1.
